channel.title: Avatar on RaihnForge
channel.description: Recent content in Avatar on RaihnForge

item.title: Commission: Gentle Avatar
item.description:
Stormbolt to the FACE – Gentle WC3’s Avatar Commission

Awesome player, streamer, and all-around good human, Gentle reached out for a custom WC3 Champions avatar—and I couldn’t have been happier to answer the call.

He was super kind and professional throughout, and one thing that really made this commission special was the involvement of his girlfriend. She’s an artist too, and the piece she created for him was not only fantastic but hugely inspiring as I explored the right angle and energy for the Mountain King.

item.title: Commission: Gentle Avatar
item.description:
Avatar commissions for W3Champions players are always personal — the avatar represents someone in a competitive community they care about. The brief for this one was clear: approachable, friendly, with strong visual presence even at small sizes.

Avatar design lives at the intersection of portraiture and icon design. It needs to work at 64×64 pixels but also hold up when displayed larger on stream or profile pages. Every element has to earn its place.

item.title: Destiny Avatar: E4 KEYEZ
item.description:
E4 KEYEZ is my Destiny character — the Guardian I’ve sunk hundreds of hours into. This piece was a personal project: building a proper avatar for the PlayStation Network profile using the character as a foundation but pushing beyond what the game renders.

The challenge was staying true to the in-game aesthetic while adding enough hand-crafted illustration to make it feel like art rather than a screenshot. The Destiny visual language is dense — lots of intricate armor detail, dramatic lighting, and a specific color palette per class.

item.title: Overlay for X-Com Stream
item.description:
A couple of weeks ago a friend of mine ‘Kayfix’ and I decided to play a run through of X-Com with ‘Field Expedient Muliplayer’.  The idea is that we run with two teams of four, and that all deaths are permanent (as stated in our code of conduct) and the we cast the xenomorphic extravaganza.

We hope to convey to those not in the know what X-Com means to those that are.  Utilizing the overlays and a fair dose of over excitement we feel like we are approaching a stream that is to at least to our satisfaction, if not to that of the general gaming public.
